title: calendula incana subsp. maderensis
intro: Calendula incana subsp. maderensis, also known as the Madeiran pot marigold, is a subspecies of the calendula plant, a type of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the island of Madeira. The calendula incana subsp. maderensis plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of using this plant include wound healing, skin protection, and soothing of irritated skin, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the calendula incana subsp. maderensis plant include infusions, salves, and creams, which can be used topically to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall skin health.
